The critical issue here is the lack of bounds checking on the offset parameter. If the caller provides an offset value equal to or exceeding len, this results in an out-of-bounds write to the data array. Additionally, the immediate freeing of b->data and the Buffer after the write operation doesn't introduce use-after-free or double-free issues here, as the freed memory isn't accessed afterward.
